title: Not Just the Military: Xi Jinping’s Other Senior Party Purge
gist: Beyond his well-documented military purge, Xi Jinping has targeted former Politburo Standing Committee member and one-time economic czar Wang Qishan — now under virtual house arrest and absent from official media since October 2023 — and systematically dismantled Wang's patronage network. (Text is a truncated teaser ending in '[...]'.)

insights.claim: Xi Jinping has gone after former Politburo Standing Committee member and one-time economic czar Wang Qishan, who now lives under virtual house arrest in Beijing and has not been referenced in Chinese official media since October 2023, though no crime has been charged.
insights.speaker: Dennis Wilder
insights.so_what: Sidelining a top economic reformer without formal charges signals intensifying elite consolidation under Xi — relevant to China policy-continuity and political-risk reads.
insights.quote: Xi has not charged Wang with any crime, but the latter lives under virtual house arrest in Beijing. Wang has not been referenced in the Chinese official media since October 2023.

insights.claim: Xi has systematically purged Wang Qishan's patronage network, including Li Xiaohong — a former personal secretary to Wang and once a top disciplinary official overseeing the China Securities Regulatory Commission — placed under disciplinary investigation last month.
insights.speaker: Dennis Wilder
insights.so_what: Purging Wang's network down to CSRC-linked officials shows the consolidation reaching into financial-regulatory ranks — a governance signal for China's markets oversight.
insights.quote: Li Xiaohong, a former personal secretary to Wang and once the most important disciplinary official overseeing the China Securities Regulatory Commission, was placed under disciplinary investigation
